theme: Per-floor dungeon archetypes (crypt, sewer, mushroom forest, bone library, plus one secret) with distinct palettes, glyph variants, and atmospheric metadata that the Whisperer's prose pool can lean into
summary: Sprint 11 gives the dungeon a SOUL. Until now every floor has been a generic rooms-and-corridors map; the same '#'/'.'/'+'/' <'/'>' glyphs everywhere, the same ambient prose, the same nothing. Sprint 11 adds **archetypes**: each floor in the World is assigned a thematic archetype (e.g. 'crypt', 'flooded_sewer', 'mushroom_forest', 'bone_library', plus at least one rare/'secret' archetype) chosen deterministically from the master seed and floor index. Each archetype carries: (1) a stable id and human-readable name; (2) a documented **glyph variant table** that may swap the floor/wall/door glyphs (e.g. crypt floors render as '.', sewer floors render as ',' or '~', mushroom-forest walls as '%', bone-library walls as ':') while preserving walkability and the Sprint-1/2 movement contract; (3) a **palette descriptor** -- a structured set of 256/truecolor hex / xterm color codes for ASCII rendering -- documented but NOT yet emitted as ANSI in the default headless output (Sprint 12 turns colour on; Sprint 11 ships the data + a pure-Python `colorize_frame` helper that returns an ANSI-coded string callers can opt into); (4) an **archetype-keyed prose tag** that the Whisperer attaches to the room_entered / first_sight / run_started events so atmospheric prose can be archetype-flavoured; and (5) an **archetype-keyed monster pool tag** that documents which monster kinds are flavour-appropriate (a thin metadata-only stub since Sprint 5 'Monsters & AI' is not in tree -- we register named pools but do not spawn). Sprint 11 also introduces a `--archetype` CLI flag for forcing a single archetype across all floors (useful for screenshots and tests) and a `--list-archetypes` flag that prints the registered archetypes and their data. The dungeon glyph grid produced by `render_frame(game)` MAY now show archetype-variant glyphs (this is the headline visual change of the sprint), but the Sprint-2 byte-determinism contract is preserved at a slightly weaker level: with the SAME seed AND the SAME archetype assignment, two processes still produce byte-identical output. Sprint-7 / Sprint-8 / Sprint-10 contracts are preserved: `--no-whisperer` is still byte-identical to a pre-Whisperer Sprint-2-style frame for the same seed (modulo the new archetype glyph swap), all existing event types remain canonical, the Whisperer adapter / panel / chronicle still work end-to-end. Sprints 3, 4, 5, 6, 9 remain not-in-tree; this sprint must NOT depend on them.
assumptions: Sprint 10 is merged in tree and PASSED 18/18 (handoff confirms): chronicle module, end_run hook, epitaph event type + prose pool entries, --name/--chronicle/--chronicle-fixed-timestamp/--no-chronicle CLI flags, 120 pytest tests passing.

Sprints 1 (foundation), 2 (dungeon generation v1), 7 (Whisperer adapter + event bus), 8 (Whispers in play / panel + first_sight + room_entered), and 10 (Chronicle + epitaph) are all merged. Sprints 3, 4, 5, 6, 9 are NOT in tree. Sprint 11 must NOT depend on FOV, combat, items, monster spawn machinery, save/load, or director nudges.

Archetypes are pure data + a thin runtime Python registry. They live in a new module (e.g., `whisperdeep/archetypes.py`) and an associated JSON resource (e.g., `whisperdeep/archetypes.json`). Adding archetypes does NOT require touching events.py, llm.py, whisperer.py public APIs, or chronicle.py public APIs.

The archetype assignment is deterministic from (master_seed, floor_index): the same (master_seed, floor_index) always yields the same archetype id. The mapping is implemented inside the World (or a small helper in archetypes.py) so that Floor.archetype is set by World.get_floor before the Floor is returned.

Glyph variants preserve walkability semantics: a wall-variant glyph (e.g., '%' for mushroom_forest) is still a Tile of kind WALL with walkable=False; a floor-variant glyph (e.g., ',' for sewer) is still a Tile of kind FLOOR with walkable=True. The TileKind enum is NOT changed; only the per-archetype glyph table differs. Bump-to-attack (when Sprint 3 lands) and movement still treat the variants identically to their base kinds.

Palettes are a documented JSON-shaped descriptor: keys for wall_fg, floor_fg, door_fg, upstairs_fg, downstairs_fg, player_fg, panel_fg, panel_bg (or similar). Values are 256-color xterm indices (0-255) AND/OR hex strings ('#aabbcc'); both are accepted at parse time. ANSI is NOT emitted by `render_frame` by default in this sprint -- a separate `colorize_frame(game) -> str` helper produces the ANSI-coded string for opt-in callers. The default headless output remains plain ASCII so Sprint-2 / Sprint-7 byte-comparison tests still work for `--no-whisperer`.

The Whisperer's offline prose pool gains archetype-flavoured entries. At minimum, each archetype has >= 4 distinct entries for room_entered and >= 4 for first_sight; the Whisperer prefers archetype-tagged entries when an archetype is in scope and falls back to the un-tagged generic pool otherwise. Determinism is preserved: same seed + same archetype assignment -> same archetype-flavoured prose chosen.

At minimum FIVE archetypes are registered: crypt, flooded_sewer, mushroom_forest, bone_library, and one 'secret' / rare archetype (e.g., 'whisperhall' or 'deep_garden'). The secret archetype is rare (low weight in the assignment distribution) but reachable on at least one observable seed/floor combination documented in tests.

Monster pool tags are metadata only in this sprint: each archetype declares a list of monster-kind strings flavour-appropriate to it (e.g., crypt -> ['ghoul', 'wight', 'crypt-rat']) but NOTHING SPAWNS them. Sprint 5 ('Monsters & AI') will consume the pools later. Tests assert the pools are present and well-formed; they do NOT assert any monster appears on a floor.

Determinism: with `--seed N --headless --no-whisperer`, the rendered grid is byte-identical across two separate processes for the same N. With `--seed N --headless` (whisperer on), stdout is also byte-identical across two processes. Different N (or a forced different `--archetype`) MAY differ in glyphs (because of the archetype variant) but BOTH runs are still deterministic per process invocation.

Pytest remains the test runner under tests/. Tests must NOT make network calls. Sprint-7/8 layering invariants are preserved; the new archetypes module imports stdlib + typing + (floor / tiles types) only -- it does NOT import llm.py, render.py, panel.py, chronicle.py, whisperer.py.

Documentation: docs/whisperdeep.md gains a Sprint-11 status banner and an 'Archetypes & Palettes' section describing the five archetypes, the glyph-variant table, the palette descriptor format, the prose-tag convention, the monster-pool stub, the determinism guarantees, and the new CLI flags.

criteria.description: A new `whisperdeep/archetypes.py` module exists and is importable. It exposes at least: (a) a `DungeonArchetype` dataclass / class with public fields/properties for `id` (str), `name` (str), `glyph_overrides` (mapping TileKind -> str), `palette` (mapping str -> str|int), `prose_tag` (str), `monster_pool` (list[str]); (b) a registry `ARCHETYPES: tuple[DungeonArchetype, ...]` (or `dict[str, DungeonArchetype]`) listing the five+ registered archetypes; (c) a public function `get_archetype(archetype_id: str) -> DungeonArchetype` that raises KeyError on unknown ids; (d) a public function `assign_archetype(master_seed: int, floor_index: int) -> DungeonArchetype` that is deterministic for the same arguments. The module imports only stdlib + typing + `whisperdeep.tiles` (for TileKind) -- it does NOT import llm/render/panel/chronicle/whisperer.
testSteps: Run `python -c "from whisperdeep.archetypes import DungeonArchetype, ARCHETYPES, get_archetype, assign_archetype"` and confirm exit code 0.
Inspect DungeonArchetype: it has the listed fields/properties accessible on an instance.

Inspect `ARCHETYPES`: it has length >= 5 and contains entries for ids 'crypt', 'flooded_sewer', 'mushroom_forest', 'bone_library' (the secret archetype id is implementation-defined but must be one of the entries).
Inspect imports in archetypes.py via AST: only stdlib + typing + whisperdeep.tiles; NO `from .llm`, `from .render`, `from .panel`, `from .chronicle`, `from .whisperer`.
criteria.threshold: Module importable; DungeonArchetype + ARCHETYPES + get_archetype + assign_archetype exist; layering preserved.

criteria.description: At least FIVE archetypes are registered with the required ids: 'crypt', 'flooded_sewer', 'mushroom_forest', 'bone_library', and at least one 'secret' / rare archetype with a documented id. Each archetype has a non-empty `name`, a non-empty `prose_tag`, a `monster_pool` list of >= 3 distinct monster-kind strings, and a `glyph_overrides` mapping that overrides AT LEAST one of {WALL, FLOOR, DOOR}. The five required ids are documented in code/docs.
testSteps: Import ARCHETYPES; collect the set of ids.
Assert {'crypt', 'flooded_sewer', 'mushroom_forest', 'bone_library'} is a subset of the id set.
Assert len(ids) >= 5 (at least one 'secret' / additional archetype is registered).
For each archetype, assert `name` is a non-empty string and `prose_tag` is a non-empty string.
For each archetype, assert `monster_pool` is a list / tuple of >= 3 distinct non-empty strings.
For each archetype, assert `glyph_overrides` is a mapping that overrides at least one of TileKind.WALL / TileKind.FLOOR / TileKind.DOOR with a non-empty single-character string.
Verify all overridden glyph values are EXACTLY one character wide (terminal cells) and are NOT one of '@' (player) -- the player glyph is reserved.
criteria.threshold: 5+ archetypes with the four named ids + a secret; each has name/prose_tag/monster_pool/glyph_overrides; player glyph not collided with.

criteria.description: `assign_archetype(master_seed, floor_index)` is deterministic and surjective across the registered archetypes for non-trivial inputs. Specifically: (a) for any (master_seed, floor_index) the same call returns the same archetype id every time; (b) varying floor_index across a small range (0..N) for a fixed master_seed produces at least 2 distinct archetype ids (so a multi-floor world isn't all one archetype); (c) varying master_seed across a small range produces at least 3 distinct archetype ids overall (so the rare archetypes are reachable); (d) the function never returns None or an unregistered id.
testSteps: For (seed=1, floor=0): call assign_archetype twice; assert the returned `.id` strings are equal.
For seed=42, iterate floor_index in 0..7 and collect returned `.id`s; assert >= 2 distinct ids appear.
For floor_index=0, iterate seeds in 1..50 and collect returned `.id`s; assert >= 3 distinct ids appear.
Across the full sweep above, assert the secret-archetype id appears at least once for SOME (seed, floor) pair (the rare archetype is reachable).
Verify every returned `.id` is in {a.id for a in ARCHETYPES}.
Verify the function NEVER returns None on any (seed, floor) in the sweep above.
criteria.threshold: Deterministic per (seed, floor); diverse across floors and seeds; secret archetype reachable in the sweep.

criteria.description: The Floor (and World) carries the assigned archetype. World.get_floor(i) returns a Floor whose `archetype` attribute is the DungeonArchetype assigned by `assign_archetype(world.master_seed, i)`. The Floor.archetype attribute is set BEFORE the Floor is returned to callers (no late-binding). Existing Floor functionality (rooms, walkable_tiles, snapshot, in_bounds, get/set, upstairs_pos, downstairs_pos) is unchanged.
testSteps: Construct `World(master_seed=1, num_floors=3)`; for each i in 0..2, call `world.get_floor(i)` and assert `floor.archetype` is a DungeonArchetype instance whose id == assign_archetype(1, i).id.
Assert two calls to `world.get_floor(0)` return the same Floor object with the same archetype (no re-assignment).
Verify Floor still exposes `.rooms`, `.walkable_tiles()`, `.snapshot()`, `.in_bounds()`, `.upstairs_pos`, `.downstairs_pos`, `.width`, `.height`, `.seed`.
Verify the snapshot tuple shape is unchanged (rows of strings of length width).
criteria.threshold: Floor.archetype is set by World.get_floor; matches assign_archetype; pre-existing Floor API intact.

criteria.description: Glyph variants render correctly. `render_frame(game)` produces a multiline string in which the wall / floor / door glyphs reflect the floor's archetype glyph_overrides where present, and fall back to the Sprint-1/2 defaults ('#', '.', '+') where the archetype does NOT override. The player glyph '@', upstairs '<', and downstairs '>' remain unchanged regardless of archetype (these are reserved). Walkability is unchanged: a tile that rendered as the wall variant is still a wall (walkable=False); the variant floor / door tiles are still walkable.
testSteps: Pick a seed S such that floor 0's archetype overrides at least the WALL glyph (e.g., assign_archetype(S, 0).glyph_overrides[TileKind.WALL] != '#'); document the seed in the test.
Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=S, whisperer=False)`; call `render_frame(game)` and split into rows.
Assert the chosen variant wall character appears in the rendered output.
Assert NO row contains the original Sprint-2 '#' character UNLESS the archetype's WALL override is '#' itself (i.e., wall glyph swap is observed).
Assert '@' is present (player) and that NO archetype overrides '@' (the player glyph is reserved).
Inspect every wall-variant cell at (x,y); assert `floor.get(x,y).walkable` is False (variant glyphs preserve walkability semantics).
Inspect every floor-variant cell; assert `floor.get(x,y).walkable` is True.
Verify upstairs '<' (if present) and downstairs '>' are NOT overridden by any archetype glyph_overrides.
criteria.threshold: Archetype glyph_overrides are applied by render_frame; player/stairs glyphs reserved; walkability preserved.

criteria.description: Determinism across processes. Two subprocess invocations of `python -m whisperdeep --seed N --headless --no-whisperer` produce byte-identical stdout for the same N. Two subprocess invocations of `python -m whisperdeep --seed N --headless` (whisperer on) produce byte-identical stdout for the same N. Different seeds produce different stdout (in either glyph rows or whisper text or both).
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` twice in subprocesses; assert byte-identical stdout.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 2 --headless --no-whisperer`; assert different stdout from seed=1.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 11 --headless` twice with whisperer on; assert byte-identical stdout (composite frame including panel).

Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 11 --headless --chronicle TMP_A.md --chronicle-fixed-timestamp 2026-06-12T00:00:00Z` and `--chronicle TMP_B.md --chronicle-fixed-timestamp 2026-06-12T00:00:00Z` in two subprocesses; assert TMP_A.md and TMP_B.md are byte-identical.
Verify the chronicle for two DIFFERENT seeds (with everything else equal) differs in at least one line.
criteria.threshold: Same-seed determinism preserved across processes; different seeds produce different output; chronicle determinism preserved.

criteria.description: A `palette` descriptor is structured and parseable. Each archetype's `palette` is a mapping with at minimum the keys: `wall_fg`, `floor_fg`, `door_fg`, `upstairs_fg`, `downstairs_fg`, `player_fg`. Each value is either an integer in [0,255] (xterm 256-color index) OR a hex string matching `^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$`. A helper `palette_to_ansi(palette, kind) -> str` (in archetypes.py or a new render helper) returns an ANSI escape sequence for the given TileKind / role. ANSI is NEVER emitted into the default `render_frame` output -- only when explicitly requested via the new `colorize_frame` helper.
testSteps: For each archetype in ARCHETYPES, assert `palette` is a mapping containing keys {wall_fg, floor_fg, door_fg, upstairs_fg, downstairs_fg, player_fg}.
For each value, assert it is either an int in [0,255] OR a hex string matching `^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$`.

Call `palette_to_ansi(archetype.palette, 'wall_fg')`; assert the result starts with the ESC character (`\x1b`) or is `''` if the helper opts to return empty for some sentinel; if non-empty, assert it ends with 'm'.
Confirm `render_frame(game)` output for a Game on any archetype contains NO ESC (`\x1b`) characters by default.

Confirm a separate `colorize_frame(game)` helper exists and returns a string that DOES contain ESC sequences and contains the same plain-text glyphs as `render_frame(game)` after ANSI-stripping (a regex like `\x1b\[[0-9;]*m` removed).
criteria.threshold: Palette has required keys with valid 256/hex values; ANSI is opt-in via colorize_frame; render_frame emits no ESC by default.

criteria.description: The Whisperer's offline prose pool gains archetype-flavoured entries. For each registered archetype `a`, the prose pool contains at minimum 4 distinct entries tagged for archetype `a` for `room_entered` AND 4 distinct entries tagged for `a` for `first_sight`. The Whisperer prefers archetype-tagged entries when the event's floor has an archetype assigned and falls back to the un-tagged generic pool otherwise. The previous (Sprint 7/8/10) un-tagged entries remain present (no regression). Implementation may store the new entries in `prose_pool.json` under sub-keys (e.g., `room_entered.crypt`) or in a separate companion JSON; either is acceptable as long as it's documented.
testSteps: Load the prose pool resource(s).
For each archetype id, assert there are >= 4 distinct non-empty archetype-tagged entries for room_entered AND >= 4 for first_sight (per the documented storage convention).

Assert the Sprint-7/8/10 generic pools (room_entered, first_sight, run_started, run_ended, descended, killed_monster, low_hp, found_item, entered_room, epitaph) all still have >= 8 distinct entries each (no regression).
Construct a Game.from_seed(seed=S, whisperer=True) where floor 0's archetype is 'crypt'; trigger a room_entered (the spawn-room one fires automatically).

Inspect the produced whisper.text; assert it equals one of the crypt-tagged room_entered entries (preferred) OR, if the Whisperer's tag-aware selection is opt-in, assert that with tag selection enabled the crypt-tagged entry is chosen.
Construct a Game whose floor 0 archetype is a different one; assert a different archetype-tagged entry is chosen for the spawn-room whisper (proves archetype-tagged selection works).
criteria.threshold: Each archetype has >= 4 room_entered + 4 first_sight tagged entries; generic pools not regressed; archetype-aware selection observable.

criteria.description: Each whisper carries the archetype tag of the floor that produced it. The Whisper dataclass / dict gains an `archetype` field (or stores the archetype id in the existing payload / metadata) populated from `floor.archetype.id` at the moment of publication. This is observable in `game.whisperer.dump()` and in the JSON written by `--dump-whispers`. When the floor has no archetype (defensive), the field is None or 'unknown' (documented).
testSteps: Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=1, whisperer=True)`; trigger a couple of whispers (run_started + room_entered fire automatically; observe_kind('skitterer') fires first_sight).
Call game.whisperer.dump() and inspect each whisper dict.
Assert each whisper dict contains an `archetype` key (or equivalent documented field) whose value equals `world.get_floor(whisper['source_floor']).archetype.id`.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --dump-whispers TMP/whispers.json`; load TMP/whispers.json and assert every whisper carries the archetype field consistent with its source_floor.
Verify a whisper produced for a floor whose archetype is, e.g., 'mushroom_forest' carries archetype=='mushroom_forest' (and not the spawn-floor's archetype).
criteria.threshold: Whisper records carry archetype id matching their source_floor's archetype; visible in dump() and --dump-whispers JSON.

criteria.description: Floor glyph snapshot is archetype-aware. `render_floor(floor)` (the existing helper) renders the floor with archetype glyph_overrides applied. `Floor.snapshot()` continues to return the TILE-KIND tuple (unchanged contract) -- the glyph variants are a rendering concern, not a state concern. A new helper `render_floor_glyphs(floor)` (or `floor.snapshot_glyphs()`) returns a tuple of tuples of glyph strings reflecting the archetype overrides; this is the snapshot tests use to verify variant rendering.
testSteps: Construct a Floor via World.get_floor for a seed where floor.archetype overrides WALL.
Call render_floor(floor); assert the variant wall glyph appears in the output.
Call floor.snapshot(); assert it still returns a tuple of tuples of TileKind.value strings ('wall', 'floor', 'door', etc.) -- the kind-snapshot is unchanged.

Call the new glyph-snapshot helper (e.g., `floor.snapshot_glyphs()` or `render_floor_glyphs(floor)`); assert it returns a tuple of tuples of single-character strings reflecting the archetype overrides.
Confirm the kind-snapshot and the glyph-snapshot have the same shape (rows x cols).
criteria.threshold: render_floor applies overrides; Floor.snapshot() kind contract unchanged; new glyph-snapshot helper exists and reflects overrides.

criteria.description: CLI flags exist to drive archetypes. At minimum: `--archetype ID` forces a single archetype across all floors of the run (overriding the seed-derived assignment); `--list-archetypes` prints a one-line-per-archetype summary (id + name + glyph overrides + monster-pool size) to stdout and exits 0 without starting a run. Both flags appear in `--help`. Forcing an unknown id exits non-zero with a clear error.
testSteps: Run `python -m whisperdeep --help` and verify the help text mentions `--archetype` and `--list-archetypes`.

Run `python -m whisperdeep --list-archetypes`; assert exit code 0, stdout has at least 5 lines each mentioning a known archetype id ('crypt', 'flooded_sewer', 'mushroom_forest', 'bone_library', plus the secret one).
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer --archetype crypt`; assert exit 0 and stdout contains the crypt archetype's wall-variant glyph (per its glyph_overrides).

Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer --archetype mushroom_forest`; assert exit 0 and stdout contains the mushroom_forest wall-variant glyph and DOES NOT contain the crypt-only wall variant (when they differ).
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --archetype not_a_real_archetype`; assert exit code != 0 AND a clear error message mentions the bad id and lists or hints at the valid ones.
criteria.threshold: --archetype forces a single archetype; --list-archetypes prints registered archetypes; both in --help; bad id fails clearly.

criteria.description: Robustness of glyph and palette overrides. Edge cases must be handled: an archetype whose glyph_overrides is empty falls back to the Sprint-1/2 defaults for ALL kinds; an archetype that overrides only DOOR still renders WALL/FLOOR with defaults; palette_to_ansi handles unknown palette keys by returning '' (empty) rather than raising; a Floor with `archetype = None` (defensive / hand-built Floor) renders with Sprint-1/2 defaults and does NOT raise.
testSteps: Construct a `DungeonArchetype` with id='_test_empty', name='Test Empty', glyph_overrides={}, palette={}, prose_tag='_test', monster_pool=['a','b','c']. (Use the public constructor; do NOT register it globally.)
Build a Floor and set floor.archetype = the empty test archetype; call render_floor(floor); assert WALL renders as '#', FLOOR as '.', DOOR as '+'.
Construct a test archetype whose glyph_overrides ONLY overrides DOOR (e.g., to '/'); set floor.archetype = it; render_floor; assert WALL is '#' and FLOOR is '.' but DOOR is '/'.
Call palette_to_ansi(archetype.palette, 'this_key_does_not_exist'); assert it returns '' (empty string) and does not raise.
Construct a Floor manually (not from World) and explicitly set floor.archetype = None; call render_floor(floor); assert no exception and Sprint-1/2 default glyphs are used.
criteria.threshold: Missing/empty overrides degrade to Sprint-1/2 defaults; palette_to_ansi tolerates unknown keys; archetype=None floors render safely.

criteria.description: Sprint 7 + 8 + 10 contracts are fully preserved. The whisper panel still renders, render_frame_with_whispers still produces composite frames, EVENT_TYPES still contains all eleven canonical types from prior sprints (run_started, run_ended, entered_room, killed_monster, low_hp, found_item, descended, first_sight, room_entered, epitaph), --no-panel / --panel-width / --no-whisperer / --dump-whispers / --name / --chronicle / --chronicle-fixed-timestamp / --no-chronicle all still work, the chronicle module + Game.end_run + epitaph behavior unchanged.
testSteps: Run `pytest tests/test_sprint07.py tests/test_sprint08.py tests/test_sprint10.py` and verify ALL prior-sprint tests pass unchanged.

Verify `python -c "from whisperdeep.events import EVENT_TYPES; assert {'run_started','run_ended','entered_room','killed_monster','low_hp','found_item','descended','first_sight','room_entered','epitaph'}.issubset(set(EVENT_TYPES))"` exits 0.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-whisperer` -> exit 0 and grid-only output.
Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --no-panel --dump-whispers TMP.json` -> exit 0; TMP.json non-empty; each entry parseable.

Run `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1 --headless --name Mara --chronicle TMP/c.md --chronicle-fixed-timestamp 2026-06-12T00:00:00Z` -> exit 0; TMP/c.md exists, starts with `# `, contains 'Mara' and 'seed: 1' (or '- seed: 1').
Verify Game.end_run is still idempotent and the epitaph event still fires.
criteria.threshold: All prior-sprint tests pass; EVENT_TYPES superset preserved; existing CLI flags unchanged; chronicle still works.

criteria.description: Sprint 1 + 2 contracts remain intact: full prior pytest suite passes; the documented glyphs from Sprint 1/2 still appear when the seed-derived archetype does not override them (or when `--archetype` selects an archetype whose overrides preserve the defaults); World multi-floor traversal still works (descend places player at upstairs landing); Floor.in_bounds / walkable / snapshot APIs unchanged.
testSteps: Run `pytest tests/test_sprint01.py tests/test_generator.py tests/test_game.py` and verify ALL pass.
Construct `Game.from_seed(seed=1, num_floors=3, whisperer=False)`; assert `game.floor.in_bounds(0,0)` is True; `game.floor.walkable(...)` works as before.
Use `game.teleport(*game.floor.downstairs_pos)` and `game.descend()`; assert the player ends up on the next floor at the upstairs position.
Verify Floor.snapshot() is still a tuple of tuples of TileKind.value strings (unchanged kind-snapshot contract).
Verify '@' (player) is rendered correctly regardless of archetype.
criteria.threshold: Sprints 1, 2 still PASS; movement / multi-floor / walkability / Floor APIs intact.

criteria.description: A new `tests/test_sprint11.py` (or equivalently named) file contains >= 14 distinct `test_*` functions covering, at minimum: archetypes module import + types (C1), at-least-five archetypes with required ids (C2), assign_archetype determinism + diversity (C3), Floor.archetype assignment (C4), glyph variant rendering + walkability preservation (C5), determinism across processes (C6), palette descriptor shape + ansi-opt-in (C7), archetype-flavoured prose pool (C8), whisper records carry archetype (C9), Floor snapshot kind/glyph distinction (C10), CLI --archetype / --list-archetypes (C11), edge-case empty/None archetype (C12), regression of prior sprints (C13/C14). All pass with no API keys and no network.
testSteps: Locate test files under tests/ whose names reference 'sprint11', 'archetype', or 'palette'.
Count distinct `test_*` function definitions; assert the count is >= 14.
Verify each scenario above is covered by at least one named test.
Run `pytest tests/` with no API keys; assert all tests pass.
Verify no Sprint-11 test imports `requests`, `httpx`, `urllib.request`, `anthropic`, or `openai` at module top-level.
criteria.threshold: >= 14 distinct Sprint-11 test functions, all passing, covering each listed scenario; no network calls.

criteria.description: Documentation (docs/whisperdeep.md and/or README) describes Sprint 11: a Sprint-11 status banner; an 'Archetypes & Palettes' section listing the five+ archetypes and their ids/names/prose_tags; a glyph-variant table showing each archetype's wall/floor/door overrides; the palette descriptor format (keys + value formats: int 0-255 OR `#rrggbb`); the prose-tag convention and how the Whisperer prefers tagged entries; the monster-pool stub note (metadata only; not yet spawning -- Sprint 5); the new CLI flags `--archetype` and `--list-archetypes`; the determinism guarantees (same seed -> same archetype assignment); and a note that ANSI colour output is opt-in via `colorize_frame` (not in default headless output) -- full colour wiring lands in Sprint 12.
testSteps: Open docs/whisperdeep.md (and README if it references the game).
Verify a Sprint-11 banner / status section exists.
Verify the five+ archetypes are listed with id, name, and prose_tag.
Verify a glyph-variant table is present (wall/floor/door overrides per archetype).
Verify the palette descriptor format is documented (keys + int/hex values).
Verify the prose-tag convention and archetype-aware Whisperer selection are described.
Verify the monster-pool stub note is present (metadata only; Sprint 5).
Verify --archetype and --list-archetypes are documented.
Verify the determinism guarantee (same (seed, floor) -> same archetype) is stated.
Verify the colour-opt-in note (ANSI via colorize_frame; full colour in Sprint 12) is present.
criteria.threshold: All ten documentation topics are present; Sprint-11 banner / status section is added.

criteria.description: Layering invariants from Sprints 7 / 8 / 10 are preserved AND the new Sprint-11 archetypes module respects them. archetypes.py imports stdlib + typing + (whisperdeep.tiles for TileKind, optionally whisperdeep.floor for type hints via TYPE_CHECKING). It does NOT import whisperdeep.llm, whisperdeep.render, whisperdeep.panel, whisperdeep.chronicle, or whisperdeep.whisperer. World / Floor MAY import archetypes (they need to assign archetype to the Floor). Game.py and CLI.py MAY import archetypes for the --archetype flag plumbing. events.py and llm.py do NOT import archetypes (the core bus and adapter contracts are unchanged).
testSteps: Open whisperdeep/archetypes.py; verify imports via AST: stdlib, typing, .tiles (or TYPE_CHECKING); NO `from .llm`, `from .render`, `from .panel`, `from .chronicle`, `from .whisperer`.
Open whisperdeep/events.py; verify it does NOT import archetypes.
Open whisperdeep/llm.py; verify it does NOT import archetypes.
Verify whisperdeep/world.py imports archetypes and uses it to assign Floor.archetype.
Verify whisperdeep/render.py either reads floor.archetype directly OR delegates to a small helper, but does NOT make any new network / LLM calls.
Verify TileKind enum is unchanged (Sprint-1 contract preserved).
Verify EVENT_TYPES is unchanged from Sprint 10 (no new event types in Sprint 11).
criteria.threshold: archetypes module is layered correctly; events/llm don't import archetypes; TileKind & EVENT_TYPES unchanged; render uses overrides without new dependencies.

outOfScope: Real ANSI colour emission in the default `render_frame` / `render_frame_with_whispers` output. The palette is data-only in this sprint; `colorize_frame` is the opt-in helper. Full colour wiring lands in Sprint 12.
Curses / TUI / per-cell colouring of the Whisperer's panel.
Spawning monsters from the per-archetype monster pool. The pool is metadata-only; actual spawning is Sprint 5 ('Monsters & AI').
FOV / fog-of-war visual variations per archetype (Sprint 3).
Items / loot tables themed per archetype (Sprint 4).
Director nudges that bias archetype selection (Sprint 9).
Cross-run persistence of which archetypes the player has seen.
Dynamic archetype generation via LLM (e.g., the Whisperer inventing a new biome at runtime). Archetypes are a closed registry in this sprint.
Rebindable keys, help overlay, ambient audio, leaderboard (Sprint 12).
Real network calls in CI / automated tests -- provider adapters remain stubbed and gated.
Sprint 3/4/5/6/9 features not in tree. The chronicle/whisperer/panel from prior sprints continue to function; but archetype-driven combat/FOV/items/director-nudges are NOT introduced here.
deliverables: A new `whisperdeep/archetypes.py` module with `DungeonArchetype` dataclass, an `ARCHETYPES` registry of >= 5 archetypes (crypt, flooded_sewer, mushroom_forest, bone_library, plus a secret), and `get_archetype` + `assign_archetype` deterministic helpers.
A `whisperdeep/archetypes.json` resource (or in-module data) carrying glyph_overrides, palette, prose_tag, monster_pool per archetype.
World.get_floor populates Floor.archetype via assign_archetype(master_seed, floor_index).

Floor gains an `archetype` attribute (default None for defensively-constructed Floors); existing Floor.snapshot kind contract unchanged; new glyph-aware snapshot helper available (`snapshot_glyphs` or `render_floor_glyphs`).
render_frame / render_floor apply archetype glyph_overrides for WALL/FLOOR/DOOR while preserving the reserved player ('@'), upstairs ('<'), and downstairs ('>') glyphs.
A `colorize_frame(game) -> str` (or equivalent) helper that emits ANSI escape sequences using the archetype palette; default `render_frame` continues to emit no ANSI.
Prose pool extension with archetype-tagged entries: >= 4 distinct entries per archetype for room_entered AND first_sight; generic pools (Sprint 7/8/10) preserved with >= 8 entries each.
Whisperer publishes whispers carrying the archetype id of the source floor (visible in `dump()` and `--dump-whispers` output).
CLI flags `--archetype ID` (force single archetype across all floors) and `--list-archetypes` (print registry summary and exit 0); both in `--help`.
>= 14 NEW pytest tests under tests/test_sprint11.py covering all C1-C12 scenarios, no network.

docs/whisperdeep.md updated with a Sprint-11 banner and an Archetypes & Palettes section covering: archetypes list, glyph-variant table, palette descriptor format, prose-tag convention, monster-pool stub note, --archetype / --list-archetypes flags, determinism guarantees, and the ANSI-opt-in (colour wiring lands in Sprint 12) note.
